title: API Response Examples
text: live CrossRef data: , runnable example uses curated subset real saved response. CrossRef date-parts entries may scalar year tuple-like year/month/day array saved response. Inference records observed array shapes, small manual edit can define reusable date component reference every matching date-parts position. path walks inferred homogeneous arrays. first rest means “schema item”; final rest means “schema date-parts entry”. reusable component clear domain name, author explicitly $defs $ref, use schema_replace_where() replace repeated logical paths shared reference. schema_compact() deliberately avoids inventing reusable definition names .

title: Introduction to schemate
text: Use validation modes according caller. examples use one small schema return shape easy compare. assert default application code. returns input invisibly success throws error failure. check returns TRUE diagnostic string. useful want show store validation message. test returns plain boolean. useful control flow. expect returns testthat-style expectation object package tests.
code: mode_schema <- schema_doc(list(     check = list(kind = "list"),     fields = list(id = schema_check("int", lower = 1)) )) mode_good <- list(id = 1L) mode_bad <- list(id = 0L) mode_schema #> { #>   "check": { #>     "kind": "list" #>   }, #>   "fields": { #>     "id": { #>       "check": { #>         "kind": "int", #>         "lower": 1 #>       } #>     } #>   } #> } mode_schema |>     schema_validate(mode_good) try(mode_schema |>     schema_validate(mode_bad, name = "payload")) #> Error : payload$id: Element 1 is not >= 1 mode_schema |>     schema_validate(mode_bad, mode = "check", name = "payload") #> [1] "payload$id: Element 1 is not >= 1" mode_schema |>     schema_validate(mode_good, mode = "test") #> [1] TRUE mode_schema |>     schema_validate(mode_bad, mode = "test") #> [1] FALSE mode_schema |>     schema_validate(mode_good, mode = "expect") #> <expectation_success/expectation/condition> #> As expected

title: Schema Inference, Editing, and Validation with checkmate
text: public API uses single schema_ prefix works well pipelines. Start example object, infer conservative schema, compact something easier edit review. schema_validate() defaults assert mode: invalid input raises error valid input returned invisibly. modes available need message boolean result. validating many payloads schema, flatten reuse flattened schema. data frame example, see Get started article.
code: library(schemate)  payload <- list(     items = list(         list(id = 1L, name = "alpha", label = "Alpha", slug = "alpha"),         list(id = 2L, name = "beta", label = "Beta", slug = "beta")     ) )  schema <- payload |>     schema_infer(keys = "named", arrays = "rest") |>     schema_compact() |>     schema_set_desc("$items", "Repository-like result items")  schema ## { ##   "check": { ##     "kind": "list" ##   }, ##   "keys": { ##     "type": "named" ##   }, ##   "fields": { ##     "items": { ##       "description": "Repository-like result items", ##       "check": { ##         "kind": "list" ##       }, ##       "keys": { ##         "type": "unnamed" ##       }, ##       "rest": { ##         "check": { ##           "kind": "list" ##         }, ##         "keys": { ##           "type": "named" ##         }, ##         "fields": { ##           "id": { ##             "check": { ##               "kind": "int" ##             } ##           } ##         }, ##         "groups": [ ##           { ##             "names": ["name", "label", "slug"], ##             "check": { ##               "kind": "string" ##             } ##           } ##         ] ##       } ##     } ##   } ## } schema |>     schema_validate(payload, mode = "test") ## [1] TRUE bad_payload <- payload bad_payload$items[[1L]]$id <- "bad"  schema |>     schema_validate(bad_payload, mode = "check", name = "payload") ## [1] "payload$items[[1]]$id: Must be of type 'single integerish value', not 'character'" schema |>     schema_validate(bad_payload, mode = "test", name = "payload") ## [1] FALSE flat <- schema_flatten(schema) schema_validate(flat, payload, mode = "test") ## [1] TRUE

title: Schema Inference, Editing, and Validation with checkmate
text: schemate closest spirit checkmate: schemas ultimately validate R objects calling checkmate checks. adds schema lifecycle around checks: infer, edit, serialize, read, validate. pointblank better fit tabular data quality workflows, reporting, column-oriented validation plans. schemate deliberately narrower structural: describes R values, R object names, nested lists, JSON-like payloads, package-facing input contracts. replacement JSON Schema jsonvalidate, better choices need standards-compliant JSON document validation. R validation ecosystem broad: validate captures data validation rules can documented, stored, applied data sets. assertr designed assertive data checks inside analysis pipelines. data.validator focuses dataset validation reporting. vetr provides template-based structural checks R objects. testthat right home unit-test expectations; schema_validate(..., mode = "expect") intended fit style.
